title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Chief Inspector Hakaste interrogates Mrs. Rivard regarding the identity of a man in a newspaper photograph. Mrs. Rivard claims the man is her husband, Harry Castleman, whom she married on May 15, 1948. She asserts that Harry has a distinctive scar behind his left ear, caused by a razor accident involving a dog in late 1948. She maintains that she has not seen him since she sent him away in 1951.

Hakaste challenges her testimony, noting that forensic examinations indicate the scar tissue is only five or six years old. He points out that if the scar is recent, the man cannot be her husband, as Harry did not have it when he left her in 1951. Hakaste warns Mrs. Rivard that providing false information could lead to charges of perjury. Although she initially reacts with indignant defiance, claiming she is merely performing her civic duty, her demeanor shifts to fear and anxiety once Hakaste leaves.

Mrs. Rivard goes to a post office and uses a phone booth to call an individual named Flo. During the call, she reveals that she was instructed to lie about the scar and had previously accepted money to do so. Despite her fear of being an accessory to a crime or facing jail time, she agrees to meet the person the following day because the offered sum of money is large enough to improve her life. She decides to proceed with the deception, planning to use excuses such as memory lapses or mistaken identity to evade police scrutiny.
